Issue: Streaming API Support for OpenAI-Compatible Chat Completions
Overview
This issue covers the implementation of streaming support for the OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint. When clients send requests with stream: true, the router should make streaming requests to providers and return Server-Sent Events (SSE) to the client.

Created: January 26, 2026
Priority: High
Estimated Time: 4-6 hours
Status: Planning

Problem Statement
Current State
The SentinelRouter currently only supports non-streaming (synchronous) requests to the /v1/chat/completions endpoint. This means:

No Streaming Support: Clients cannot receive incremental responses as they are generated by the LLM provider.
Poor User Experience: For long responses, clients must wait for the entire response before seeing any output.
Incompatible with OpenAI SDK: The OpenAI SDK and compatible clients expect streaming support via the stream: true parameter.
Requirements
Detect Streaming Requests: Identify when stream: true is present in the request body.
Streaming Provider Calls: Make streaming requests to the selected provider and yield chunks as they arrive.
SSE Response Format: Return responses in Server-Sent Events format (data: {...}\n\n).
Error Handling: Handle provider errors mid-stream by sending SSE error events to the client.
Connection Management: Properly handle client disconnects, timeouts, and cleanup of provider connections.
No Escalation on Streaming: If a streaming request fails, return an error to the client (do not attempt provider escalation mid-stream).
Out of Scope
Sentinel evaluation on streaming responses (Phase 2)
Token-based rate limiting for streaming (current rate limiting is request-count based)
Mid-stream provider escalation
Architecture Changes
1. API Layer Changes
File: src/api/routes/chat.py (or equivalent chat completions endpoint)

Detect stream: true in the request body
Return fastapi.responses.StreamingResponse with media_type="text/event-stream"
Set appropriate headers: Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive, X-Accel-Buffering: no
2. Provider Client Changes
Files: Provider client implementations (e.g., src/providers/openai_client.py, src/providers/anthropic_client.py, etc.)

Add async generator method stream_chat_completions() to each provider client
Use httpx.AsyncClient.stream() or provider SDK streaming methods
Yield OpenAI-compatible chunk objects as they arrive
Handle provider-specific streaming formats and convert to OpenAI-compatible format
3. Router/Orchestrator Changes
File: src/router/ or src/services/ (routing logic)

Add streaming-aware routing that selects a provider but does not attempt escalation
Pass streaming flag through the routing pipeline
Create async generator wrapper that handles the streaming flow
4. Response Models
File: src/models/ or src/schemas/

Add Pydantic models for streaming chunk responses:
ChatCompletionChunk
ChatCompletionChunkChoice
ChatCompletionChunkDelta
5. Error Handling
Define SSE error event format for mid-stream errors
Ensure provider connection errors are caught and converted to SSE error events
Properly close connections on client disconnect
Implementation Plan
Phase 1: Add Streaming Response Models (0.5 hours)
Step 1.1: Create Pydantic models for OpenAI-compatible streaming chunks.

Phase 2: Implement Provider Streaming Methods (2 hours)
Step 2.1: Add stream_chat_completions() async generator method to the base provider client interface.

Step 2.2: Implement streaming for each provider:

OpenAI/OpenRouter: Use stream=True parameter, parse SSE chunks
Anthropic: Use Anthropic SDK streaming, convert to OpenAI format
Groq: Use Groq SDK streaming, convert to OpenAI format
DeepSeek: Use streaming endpoint, convert to OpenAI format
Step 2.3: Each provider's streaming method should:

Accept the same parameters as non-streaming
Yield ChatCompletionChunk objects
Handle provider-specific error responses
Clean up connections on completion or error
Phase 3: Update API Endpoint (1 hour)
Step 3.1: Modify the chat completions endpoint to detect streaming requests.

Step 3.2: Create async generator function that:

Selects provider using existing routing logic
Calls provider's streaming method
Formats chunks as SSE events (data: {json}\n\n)
Yields data: [DONE]\n\n on completion
Catches exceptions and yields SSE error events
Step 3.3: Return StreamingResponse with proper headers.

Phase 4: Connection Management (1 hour)
Step 4.1: Implement client disconnect detection using FastAPI's Request.is_disconnected().

Step 4.2: Add timeout handling for long-running streams (configurable, e.g., 5 minutes).

Step 4.3: Ensure provider connections are properly closed when:

Client disconnects
Stream completes normally
Error occurs
Timeout is reached
Step 4.4: Use try/finally blocks to guarantee cleanup.

Phase 5: Error Handling (0.5 hours)
Step 5.1: Define error event format for SSE:

Step 5.2: Catch and handle:

Provider connection errors
Provider API errors (rate limits, auth failures)
Timeout errors
Malformed response errors
Step 5.3: Log errors appropriately for debugging.

Phase 6: Testing (1 hour)
Step 6.1: Add unit tests for streaming response models.

Step 6.2: Add unit tests for provider streaming methods (with mocked responses).

Step 6.3: Add integration tests that verify:

SSE format is correct
Chunks are yielded incrementally
[DONE] is sent at end
Errors are properly formatted
Client disconnect is handled
Step 6.4: Manual testing with curl:

Technical Specifications
SSE Response Format
Each chunk should be formatted as:

Final message:

Headers Required
Header	Value	Purpose
Content-Type	text/event-stream	SSE MIME type
Cache-Control	no-cache	Prevent caching
Connection	keep-alive	Keep connection open
X-Accel-Buffering	no	Disable nginx buffering
Timeout Configuration
Default stream timeout: 300 seconds (5 minutes)
Should be configurable via environment variable STREAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
Files to Create/Modify
File	Action	Description
src/models/streaming.py	Create	Streaming chunk Pydantic models
src/providers/base.py	Modify	Add abstract stream_chat_completions() method
src/providers/openai_client.py	Modify	Implement streaming for OpenAI
src/providers/anthropic_client.py	Modify	Implement streaming for Anthropic
src/providers/groq_client.py	Modify	Implement streaming for Groq
src/providers/deepseek_client.py	Modify	Implement streaming for DeepSeek
src/providers/openrouter_client.py	Modify	Implement streaming for OpenRouter
src/api/routes/chat.py	Modify	Add streaming detection and response
src/services/streaming.py	Create	Streaming orchestration logic
tests/unit/test_streaming.py	Create	Unit tests for streaming
tests/integration/test_streaming_e2e.py	Create	Integration tests for streaming
Testing Plan
Unit Tests
Streaming Models: Verify chunk models serialize correctly to OpenAI format
Provider Streaming: Mock provider responses and verify chunks are yielded correctly
SSE Formatting: Verify data: {json}\n\n format is correct
Error Handling: Verify errors are converted to SSE error events
Integration Tests
End-to-End Streaming: Send streaming request, verify chunks arrive incrementally
Client Disconnect: Start stream, disconnect client, verify cleanup occurs
Provider Error: Mock provider error mid-stream, verify error event is sent
Timeout: Configure short timeout, verify timeout error is sent
Manual Testing
Acceptance Criteria
✅ Streaming Detection
 Requests with stream: true return StreamingResponse
 Requests with stream: false or missing stream use existing non-streaming logic
✅ SSE Format
 Each chunk is formatted as data: {json}\n\n
 Stream ends with data: [DONE]\n\n
 Content-Type header is text/event-stream
✅ Provider Streaming
 All providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, DeepSeek, OpenRouter) support streaming
 Provider-specific formats are converted to OpenAI-compatible format
✅ Error Handling
 Provider errors mid-stream send SSE error event
 Connection errors are handled gracefully
 Errors are logged for debugging
✅ Connection Management
 Client disconnect stops the stream and cleans up provider connection
 Stream timeout is configurable and enforced
 No resource leaks on any termination path
✅ Compatibility
 Works with curl SSE requests
 Works with OpenAI Python SDK stream=True
 Works with OpenAI Node.js SDK streaming
Rollback Plan
If streaming causes issues:

Feature Flag: Add ENABLE_STREAMING=false environment variable to disable streaming
Return Error: When streaming is disabled, return 400 error for stream: true requests
Revert Code: If necessary, revert the streaming-related commits

Dependencies
httpx with async streaming support (already in project)
Provider SDKs with streaming support
FastAPI StreamingResponse
Related Issues
CI/CD Pipeline Implementation (issues_ci_cd.md) - Tests should include streaming tests
Enhanced Routing Decision Tracking - Streaming requests should be tracked
Notes
No Escalation: Streaming requests do not support provider escalation. If the selected provider fails, the error is returned to the client.
Rate Limiting: Current request-count based rate limiting works unchanged for streaming requests.
